task_completion.description: Tor Browser settings are modified to allow localhost connections
task_completion.visual_evidence: Tor Browser is open, and the 'about:config' page or settings are visible showing modifications to allow localhost connections (e.g., 'network.proxy.allow_hijacking_localhost' set to false, or 'network.proxy.no_proxies_on' modified to include 127.0.0.1).

task_completion.description: A listener is started to capture the HTTP request
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window shows a command like 'nc -l -p 8080' or 'nc -l 8080' running to listen for incoming connections on port 8080.

task_completion.description: Tor Browser navigates to the target local URL
task_completion.visual_evidence: Tor Browser's address bar shows 'http://127.0.0.1:8080/tor-dev-test' and an attempt to load the page.

task_completion.description: Raw HTTP headers are captured from the browser request
task_completion.visual_evidence: The terminal running the listener displays the incoming raw HTTP request, which must include the line 'GET /tor-dev-test HTTP/1.1' along with other standard browser headers (like Host, User-Agent, Accept).

task_completion.description: Captured headers are saved to the correct file path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal or text editor shows the captured HTTP headers saved to the file '/home/ga/Documents/tor_headers.txt' (e.g., via a 'cat' command, redirection in the terminal, or an open text editor).

integrity.description: The captured headers must come from a genuine browser request
integrity.visual_evidence: The captured text should contain multiple standard HTTP headers (Host, User-Agent, Accept-Language, etc.) typical of a web browser, not just a single manually typed line or a simple 'curl' request.

integrity.description: Tor Browser must be used to make the request
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser shown navigating to the URL must be Tor Browser (identifiable by its UI, onion logo, or specific User-Agent), not standard Firefox or Chrome.

integrity.description: The agent must not manually type the expected output
integrity.visual_evidence: The file '/home/ga/Documents/tor_headers.txt' should be populated by redirecting the output of the listener or copying the actual captured terminal output, rather than the agent manually typing 'GET /tor-dev-test HTTP/1.1' into a text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path for the captured HTTP headers is /home/ga/Documents/tor_headers.txt. The target testing URL is http://127.0.0.1:8080/tor-dev-test, and the expected standard HTTP GET request line is GET /tor-dev-test HTTP/1.1.
